{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether any medications are available to prevent COVID-19 following exposure (post-exposure prophylaxis). Reviewing the documents, several sources specifically mention medications for post-exposure prophylaxis. Document [3] discusses bamlanivimab and etesevimab for post-exposure prophylaxis in high-risk individuals. Document [10] states that the FDA authorized bamlanivimab and etesevimab for emergency use as post-exposure prophylaxis in adults. Document [18] confirms that both bamlanivimab-etesevimab and casirivimab-imdevimab have been granted EUA for post-exposure prophylaxis. Document [23] notes that as of 2023, the only monoclonal antibody with active EUA is tixagevimab and cilgavimab, which is authorized for preexposure prophylaxis, but the question is about post-exposure. Although some drugs are no longer authorized due to reduced efficacy against Omicron variants (e.g., casirivimab-imdevimab, bamlanivimab-etesevimab as of Jan 2022), the documents indicate that at least some medications were available for post-exposure prophylaxis.
